steps.title: Use the sample-size threshold
steps.content: The meta-analysis says researchers need at least 176 individuals to be confident that a finding of population-level handedness is not statistical noise. The 1993 gorilla study included only 2 individuals, far below that benchmark. A two-individual sample can show what those gorillas did, but not a reliable population pattern overall with confidence.

steps.title: Reject other explanations
steps.content: Choice B invents different handedness standards. Choice C discusses the 1963 chimpanzee study rather than the 1993 gorilla claim. Choice D claims right-handedness is rare among gorillas, which the passage never states. The answer is A.
